description: Canonical doc-first profile with TASK.md as the primary artifact.

label: Devin
description: Doc-first task profile for delegating implementation or investigation work to Devin through the ravi devin CLI.

inputs.label: Max ACU
inputs.description: Explicit max ACU ceiling for the remote Devin session.

inputs.label: Repo
inputs.description: Repository passed to Devin when relevant.

completion.notes: Include the Devin session id/url, synced status, PR/artifacts if any, validation performed, and remaining gaps.

templates.create:
## Devin Task Created

Task: {{task.id}}
Title: {{task.title}}
Status: {{task.status}}
Readiness: {{readiness.label}}
Priority: {{task.priority}}
Profile: {{profile.id}}@{{profile.version}}
Surface: {{profile.rendererHints.label}}
Primary artifact: {{create.primaryArtifactLabel}} :: {{create.primaryArtifactPath}}

## Write A Devin-Ready Brief Before Dispatch

Before dispatching, make TASK.md explicit enough for an external remote executor. Include:

- Objective: the concrete outcome expected.
- Source of truth: specs, files, tickets, branches, repos, and docs Devin must read first.
- Scope in: what Devin should implement or investigate now.
- Scope out: what Devin must not touch.
- Repo/branch: which repository and branch to work from.
- Devin mode: recommended `advanced_mode` (`create`, `analyze`, `improve`, `batch`, or `manage`).
- ACU ceiling: explicit `--max-acu` or why `--no-max-acu-limit` is intentional.
- Constraints: no secrets in prompts, no real external actions unless approved, no daemon restart unless requested.
- Validation: commands, checks, smoke tests, examples, and expected output.
- Handoff: what Devin must report back, including session id/url, changed files, PRs, artifacts, failures, and gaps.

## Useful Commands

```bash
ravi devin auth check
ravi devin sessions create --advanced-mode {{input.advancedMode}} --max-acu {{input.maxAcu}} --repo {{input.repo}} --title "..." --prompt-file /path/to/prompt.md --json
ravi devin sessions show <session> --sync --json
ravi devin sessions messages <session> --json
ravi devin sessions send <session> "follow-up" --json
ravi devin sessions sync <session> --artifacts --json
```

## Original Instructions

{{task.instructions}}

## Next

{{create.nextSteps}}
templates.resume:
[System] The daemon restarted. Continue Devin-backed task {{task.id}} ("{{task.title}}") from where you stopped.
Progress: {{task.progress}}% | profile: {{profile.id}} | artifact: {{task.taskDocPath}}
Operational context:
- effective session cwd: {{session.cwd}}
- contextual worktree: {{worktree.label}}

Resume protocol:
1. Open TASK.md and find the Devin session id/url.
2. Sync remote state: ravi devin sessions show <session> --sync --json
3. If needed, fetch messages: ravi devin sessions messages <session> --json
4. Continue monitoring, sending follow-up, or syncing artifacts.
5. Update TASK.md and sync Ravi task state with report|done|block|fail.
templates.dispatchSummary: The target session was instructed to delegate the task to Devin through `ravi devin`, then monitor/sync the remote result back into Ravi.
templates.dispatchEventMessage: Dispatched to {{session.agentId}}/{{session.name}} with profile {{profile.id}}. Delegation protocol: edit {{task.taskDocPath}}, create a Devin session with `ravi devin sessions create`, record the returned session id/url, monitor with show/messages/sync, then update task state via ravi tasks report|done|block|fail {{task.id}}.
